# Wasilah

> Wasilah is a free Islamic companion app for Muslims in Bangladesh and Bengali-speaking Muslims worldwide. It brings together accurate local prayer times, Adhan, Qibla direction, Quran, Hadith, duas, the 99 Names of Allah, a Zakat calculator in Bangladeshi Taka, and a Hajj and Umrah guide. Live on the App Store and Google Play. Built by Aykiz Intelligence.

Key facts:

- Free to download and use. No paywalls, and the app will never be monetized.
- Available in English, Bangla, and Arabic.
- Designed for Bangladesh: dynamic local prayer times, Jumu'ah shown on Fridays, Zakat calculated in BDT, and full Bangla content for Quran, Hadith, and duas.
- Works offline-first. Prayer times and Qibla are computed on-device.
- iOS: https://apps.apple.com/app/id6767919573
- Android: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=site.wasilah.wasilah
- Publisher: Aykiz Intelligence (https://aykizintelligence.com/), the studio of Miraz Mullick.
- Contact: hello@wasilah.site (general and support), privacy@wasilah.site (privacy), legal@wasilah.site (legal).

## Pages

- [Download Wasilah](https://wasilah.site/get/): direct links to both stores.
- [Wasilah for iPhone and iPad](https://wasilah.site/ios/): iOS feature overview and FAQ.
- [Wasilah for Android](https://wasilah.site/android/): Android feature overview and FAQ.
- [Prayer times in Bangladesh](https://wasilah.site/blog/prayer-times-bangladesh/): guide to how prayer times are calculated for Bangladesh.
- [Zakat in Bangladeshi Taka](https://wasilah.site/blog/zakat-bangla-taka/): guide to calculating Zakat in BDT.
- [Tahajjud prayer guide (Bangla)](https://wasilah.site/blog/tahajjud-namaz-niyom/): how and when to pray Tahajjud, in Bangla.
- [Istikhara prayer and dua (Bangla)](https://wasilah.site/blog/istikhara-namaz-niyom/): how to perform Salatul Istikhara, in Bangla.
- [Ayatul Kursi virtues (Bangla)](https://wasilah.site/blog/ayatul-kursi-fazilat/): meaning, virtues and daily practice of Ayatul Kursi, in Bangla.
- [Jumu'ah day deeds (Bangla)](https://wasilah.site/blog/jumar-diner-amol/): Sunnah acts and virtues of Friday, in Bangla.
- [Learn salah for beginners (Bangla)](https://wasilah.site/blog/namaz-shikhar-niyom/): step-by-step guide to learning the prayer, in Bangla.
- [Darood Sharif virtues (Bangla)](https://wasilah.site/blog/darood-sharif-fazilat/): virtues and etiquette of sending salawat, in Bangla.
- [Privacy policy](https://wasilah.site/privacy/)

## FAQ

Q: Is Wasilah free?
A: Yes. Wasilah is free to download and use, with no ads in the prayer or Quran experience, and it will never be monetized.

Q: What does Wasilah include?
A: Accurate local prayer times, Adhan, Qibla direction, the full Quran, Hadith, duas, the 99 Names of Allah, a Zakat calculator in Bangladeshi Taka (BDT), and a Hajj and Umrah guide.

Q: What languages does Wasilah support?
A: English, Bangla, and Arabic, with full Bangla content for Quran, Hadith, and duas.

Q: Who is Wasilah for?
A: Muslims in Bangladesh and Bengali-speaking Muslims worldwide. It is designed for Bangladesh, with dynamic local prayer times, Jumu'ah shown on Fridays, and Zakat in BDT.

Q: Does Wasilah work offline?
A: Yes. Prayer times and Qibla are computed on-device and work offline once your location is set; Quran, Hadith, duas, the 99 Names, and the guides are bundled in the app. Audio recitation streams, so that part needs a connection.

Q: What data does Wasilah collect?
A: Wasilah does not collect personal data and does not track you. Your location is used on-device to compute prayer times and Qibla and is not sent anywhere. See https://wasilah.site/privacy/.

Q: How do I download Wasilah?
A: Free on the App Store (https://apps.apple.com/app/id6767919573) and Google Play (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=site.wasilah.wasilah), or via https://wasilah.site/get/.
